title: SACRAMENTO – Today, Assemblyman Jeff Gonzalez (R-Indio) issued the following statement after AB 1016 passed out of the Assembly Utilities and Energy Committee:
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 1016 increases the generating capacity of geothermal power plants eligible for local permitting.
The bill aims to unlock California’s geothermal potential.
AB 1016 could lead to the creation of quality-paying jobs in regions like Imperial County.
The unemployment rate in Imperial County is 17.2%, the highest in the state.
By streamlining the path for clean, reliable base-load power, AB 1016 positions Imperial County to become a hub of clean energy innovation.
The implementation of AB 1016 supports California’s climate and energy goals.
AB 1016 is intended to strengthen the grid and build a more resilient, sustainable future for Californians.
